senators.name: Bernie Moreno
senators.notNerdReason: B.A. from American University and M.B.A. from University of Texas. Career as auto dealership owner and executive. Business background provides practical skills but legislative approach not analytically driven. Defeated Sherrod Brown in 2024 with Trump backing.

senators.name: Jon Husted
senators.notNerdReason: B.A. from Ohio State University and J.D. from University of Dayton School of Law. Career as attorney, Ohio Secretary of State (election administration), and Lieutenant Governor. Legal and administrative background with solid policy expertise but not distinctly analytical or evidence-based.
education: J.D., University of Dayton School of Law
experience: U.S. Senator since 2025 (appointed to fill JD Vance's seat)

OH-01.name: Greg Landsman
OH-01.notNerdReason: B.A. from Tulane University and M.A. from University of Cincinnati. Career in education policy and Cincinnati City Council. Strong policy focus on education and housing but approach is more advocacy-driven than analytically rigorous.

OH-02.name: David Taylor
OH-02.notNerdReason: New member (2025). Career in state legislature and conservative politics. Limited public record of analytical or evidence-based governance approach.

OH-03.name: Joyce Beatty
OH-03.notNerdReason: B.A. from Central State University, M.S. from Wright State University, and additional graduate work at Ohio State University. Career in state legislature and university administration. Policy background but approach not distinctly analytical or research-driven.
experience: Senior Vice President, Ohio State University

OH-04.name: Jim Jordan
OH-04.notNerdReason: B.S. in economics from University of Wisconsin, M.A. from Ohio State University, and law student at Capital University (did not complete degree). Career as wrestling coach and politician. Chair of House Judiciary Committee. Legislative approach is aggressively ideological with no demonstrated evidence-based or analytical policy framework.

OH-05.name: Bob Latta
OH-05.notNerdReason: B.A. from Bowling Green State University and J.D. from University of Toledo College of Law. Career as attorney and state legislator. Legal training provides analytical skills. Serves on Energy and Commerce Committee. Solid policy engagement but approach not distinctly evidence-based.
education: J.D., University of Toledo College of Law

OH-06.name: Michael Rulli
OH-06.notNerdReason: Career as businessman and Ohio state legislator. Business background. Limited public record of analytical or evidence-based policy approach.

OH-07.name: Max Miller
OH-07.notNerdReason: B.A. from Kent State University. Career as Trump White House aide and political operative. No analytical or evidence-based policy background.
experience: White House Aide to President Trump

OH-08.name: Warren Davidson
OH-08.nerdReason: B.S. from U.S. Military Academy at West Point and M.B.A. from Notre Dame. Career as Army Ranger with six years of service, then manufacturing entrepreneur. West Point + Notre Dame MBA represents rigorous analytical training. Founded a manufacturing company. Brings military analytical discipline and business acumen to fiscal and defense policy.
education: B.S., U.S. Military Academy at West Point

OH-09.name: Marcy Kaptur
OH-09.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Wisconsin-Milwaukee and M.A. in urban planning from University of Michigan. Longest-serving congresswoman in U.S. history. Deep expertise in agriculture, energy, and manufacturing policy built over decades. Urban planning degree provides quantitative spatial analysis training. Exceptional policy depth but approach is more experiential than analytically rigorous.
education: M.A. Urban Planning, University of Michigan

OH-10.name: Mike Turner
OH-10.nerdReason: B.A. from Ohio Northern University, J.D. from Case Western Reserve University, and M.B.A. from University of Dayton. Three degrees across law, business, and political science. Former Chairman of the House Permanent Select Committee on Intelligence. Deep intelligence and defense policy expertise. Analytical approach to national security built over decades on intelligence and armed services committees.

OH-11.name: Shontel Brown
OH-11.notNerdReason: B.A. from Cuyahoga Community College. Career in county government. No analytical or evidence-based policy background.

OH-12.name: Troy Balderson
OH-12.notNerdReason: B.S. from Ohio State University. Career as car dealership owner and state legislator. Business background. No analytical or evidence-based policy approach.

OH-13.name: Emilia Sykes
OH-13.notNerdReason: B.A. from Howard University and J.D. from University of Akron School of Law. Career as attorney and state legislator (Minority Leader of Ohio House). Legal training provides analytical foundation. Deep policy engagement on healthcare, education, and criminal justice. Solid but approach not distinctly evidence-based.
education: J.D., University of Akron School of Law
experience: Minority Leader, Ohio House of Representatives

OH-14.name: David Joyce
OH-14.notNerdReason: B.A. from University of Dayton and J.D. from Cleveland-Marshall College of Law. Career as prosecutor (Geauga County Prosecutor for 17 years). Legal and prosecutorial background provides analytical skills. Solid bipartisan approach but governance not distinctly research-driven.

OH-15.name: Mike Carey
OH-15.notNerdReason: B.S. from Ohio State University. Career as coal industry lobbyist. Energy industry background. No analytical or evidence-based policy approach.
